segments.text: noses obviously they are risky areas but the most the biggest risk is actually above halfway so from Little Bella up is the comes from the internal carotid whereas most the blood supply where we're going to Jackson from the external crotch of blood supply from the facial artery and the lateral nasal artery which means if we did have a problem it'll be it should be localized and obviously what we worry about the most Is Blindness um or even worse a stroke which is really almost incredibly rare and almost impossible with the amount of product we use but it's on the list so um it's reassuring to know that in the lower third of the nodes you're just far less likely to cause that it's theoretically possible anyway because all these vessels are connected um but um we are safer than where we would be injecting a normal non-surgical nose job I'm also going to point the needle in the opposite direction as much as I can so the flow of my product is into a in theory in a more safe Direction and we'll aspirate a lot I'm using a product that aspirates extremely well

I've tested it it's one of the best aspirating products during low lift a softer G Prime product also protects me from one of the side effects which may occur which is pressure uh causing redness so if you get redness that doesn't go away that could be that there's just a little bit too much pressure and the way your body accommodates is by dilating the vessels and that's a bit more likely with a thicker product

transcript: noses obviously they are risky areas but the most the biggest risk is actually above halfway so from Little Bella up is the comes from the internal carotid whereas most the blood supply where we're going to Jackson from the external crotch of blood supply from the facial artery and the lateral nasal artery which means if we did have a problem it'll be it should be localized and obviously what we worry about the most Is Blindness um or even worse a stroke which is really almost incredibly rare and almost impossible with the amount of product we use but it's on the list so um it's reassuring to know that in the lower third of the nodes you're just far less likely to cause that it's theoretically possible anyway because all these vessels are connected um but um we are safer than where we would be injecting a normal non-surgical nose job I'm also going to point the needle in the opposite direction as much as I can so the flow of my product is into a in theory in a more safe Direction and we'll aspirate a lot I'm using a product that aspirates extremely well I've tested it it's one of the best aspirating products during low lift a softer G Prime product also protects me from one of the side effects which may occur which is pressure uh causing redness so if you get redness that doesn't go away that could be that there's just a little bit too much pressure and the way your body accommodates is by dilating the vessels and that's a bit more likely with a thicker product
